qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Master Key Exposure in GraphQL Playground HTML Response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns ParseGraphQLServer.applyPlayground embedding the master key in GraphQL Playground HTML, not the Google auth adapter JWT verification path or an alg:none/RS256 algorithm-confusion root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LDAP Injection via User-Controlled groupFilter Replacement
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unsanitized LDAP groupFilter replacement in src/Adapters/Auth/ldap.js, not Google auth token verification, JWT header algorithm trust, or the advisory's alg:none authentication bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. GHSA-4q3h-vp4r-prv2 is account takeover through Google auth JWT algorithm confusion, where a forged Google token with alg:none is accepted because the adapter trusted the JWT header instead of hardcoding RS256. The qualifying findings cover unrelated GraphQL master-key disclosure and LDAP filter injection paths.
evidence: Advisory summary and details identify Parse Server Google auth adapter JWT algorithm confusion using alg:none, patched by hardcoding RS256 and replacing the custom key fetcher with jwks-rsa.
graphql_master_key_exposure/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js:208 embedding X-Parse-Master-Key in Playground HTML.
ldap_groupfilter_injection/vulnerability_report.json identifies src/Adapters/Auth/ldap.js:143 replacing {{id}} in LDAP groupFilter without escaping.

.judge/input_manifest.json lists only graphql_master_key_exposure and ldap_groupfilter_injection as final qualifying_vuln findings; neither references the Google auth adapter, JWT verification, alg:none, RS256, jwks-rsa, or equivalent source-to-sink path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 do not apply.
